title: Home thread: agent next-step suggestion (Tab-to-accept)
prompt:
GOAL: In the home (sidebar) chat, besides self-titling, let the thread's OWN agent surface a suggested NEXT STEP at the end of a turn, rendered as a CLICKABLE BUTTON. Clicking the button sends that suggestion text as the next user message to the thread's agent so it proceeds to the next step automatically; the user can also just ignore it. (NOT a Tab/ghost-text affordance — Tab is Claude Code's own key; this is a plain clickable suggestion chip/button.) This REUSES the just-shipped home-thread self-titling pattern — do not invent a parallel mechanism.

EXISTING FOUNDATION TO REUSE (already on main):
- src/commands/home-thread.ts — 'kanban home-thread set-title' CLI: resolves the thread from KANBAN_SESSION_TASK_ID env (or --session-id), routes to the store, returns the standard runCliCommand envelope. ADD a sibling verb next to it.
- src/session/home-thread-registry.ts — pure thread record ops + titleSource auto/manual. RuntimeHomeChatThread / RuntimeHomeChatThreadTitleSource in src/core/api-contract.ts (~line 300-352).
- The append-system-prompt mechanism that scopes instructions to home/sidebar agent sessions (same one that tells the agent to set-title).
- KANBAN_SESSION_TASK_ID_ENV / KANBAN_SESSION_WORKSPACE_ID_ENV injected into the session env (src/terminal/hook-runtime-context.ts).
- Frontend: web-ui/src/components/home-agent/ (home-sidebar-agent-panel.tsx composer/chat send path, home-thread-bar.tsx), use-home-threads.ts, use-home-agent-session.ts, the granular runtime store (memory 'perf10' — subscribe the new slice in the LEAF component that renders the chat/composer).

REQUIRED WORK:
BACKEND/CLI/contract
1. New CLI verb 'kanban home-thread suggest-next "<text>"' (mirror set-title: env-resolved session id or --session-id, runCliCommand envelope, register in cli-schema.ts).
2. Store the suggestion as a TRANSIENT per-thread 'pendingNextStep' (string | null) — add to the thread record + contract. It is EPHEMERAL: a new suggest-next overwrites it; it is CLEARED when the user sends a message in that thread (whether by clicking the suggestion or typing their own) and when the thread's agent starts a new turn. Persist lightly (survives a refresh) but it is not conversation history. Flow it to the UI over the SAME broadcast the title uses.
3. Append a system-prompt instruction (scoped to home/sidebar sessions, alongside the set-title instruction): at the end of a turn, if there's an obvious next action the user is likely to want, propose exactly ONE concise, self-contained next-step prompt via 'kanban home-thread suggest-next'. It must read as a ready-to-send user message (because clicking it sends it verbatim). Keep it short and actionable; omit it when there's no clear next step.

FRONTEND
4. Render pendingNextStep as a CLICKABLE suggestion button/chip near the bottom of the chat (e.g. just above the composer), styled per AGENTS.md ui primitives (Button ghost/secondary variant, surface tokens, optionally a lucide arrow/sparkle icon). Show only when a pendingNextStep exists for the active thread.
5. Clicking the button SENDS the suggestion text as the next user message through the EXISTING thread send path (same as typing it and hitting enter) so the agent proceeds automatically. After sending, clear the chip. The user may ignore it; it also clears/replaces when the agent supersedes it or the user sends their own message.
6. Live-update when the backend broadcasts a new pendingNextStep; remove the chip locally on send.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Scope to home/sidebar threads only (not task-running agents).
- Follow AGENTS.md web-ui rules (Tailwind v4, Radix, lucide, surface tokens, src/components/ui, react-use). No client-side LLM call — the suggestion text comes from the agent via the CLI.
- Backward-compat: pendingNextStep optional; existing threads.json loads cleanly.

ACCEPTANCE:
- After an agent turn, when there's an obvious next step, a clickable suggestion button appears above the composer; clicking it sends that text to the agent and the agent continues; ignoring it does nothing.
- The chip clears when the user sends (by click or typing) or the agent supersedes it; no clear next step => no chip.
- 'kanban home-thread suggest-next' works from inside a session using only injected env and returns the standard envelope.
- web:typecheck + biome clean. No any types.
